message: Failed agent attempts in --json mode write two JSON documents to stdout: the run-only result document (agent_attempt.go:159, now carrying next: []) followed by the pactum.error.v1 envelope from app.go:112 (only gateProcessError is exempted). Plain json.Unmarshal of stdout fails. Same pattern in review run / review fix / contract draft / clarify suggest failure paths. Pre-existing at HEAD; advisory.
evidence: git show HEAD:internal/app/agent_attempt.go has the identical run-only write before returning processExitError; app.go:107-115 special-cases only gateProcessError before writeErrorEnvelope.

message: New docs claim read-only show commands return pactum.not_ready.v1, but pactum prompt show --json emits promptShowNotBuiltResponse with no schema field (internal/app/prompt.go:183-190). Agents branching on the documented schema will not match the prompt show not-built response. The doc sentence is introduced by this change.
evidence: promptShowNotBuiltResponse struct fields are run_id, run_status, ready, message, suggested_command, fix — no Schema; the other show commands go through writeNotReady which sets schema.

message: classifyErrorCode's fallback `strings.Contains(msg, "not found") -> run_not_found` misclassifies non-run lookup failures as the contract-pinned stable code. With --json, `clarify answer` with a bad question id (clarify.go:183), `review finding resolve` with a bad finding id (review.go:502), `contract accept` with no draft proposal (contract_draft.go:253), and `review fix apply` with a bad attempt id (review_fix_outcomes.go:159) all emit error.code=run_not_found while the run exists. Since this slice typed every genuine run-not-found path, the fallback now fires only for these misleading cases; agents told to trust error.code get the wrong recovery signal.
evidence: Verified full path: fmt.Errorf("question not found: %s") is not a preconditionError, so app.go:107 -> writeErrorEnvelope -> classifyErrorCode matches the "not found" substring. Marked non-blocking because the fallback classifier pre-dates this slice (q_007 rationale: run_not_found test coverage already existed at HEAD, before typed errors existed).

message: Failed agent-stage commands in --json mode write two concatenated JSON documents to stdout: the run-only result JSON (agent_attempt.go:158-167 calls writeAgentAttemptRunOnly before returning processExitError), then the top-level pactum.error.v1 envelope (app.go:112, which exempts only gateProcessError). Affects execute run, review run, review fix run, contract draft on agent failure; whole-stdout JSON parsing fails, undermining the convention the updated skill/docs now instruct agents to rely on.
evidence: Pre-existing: app.go is absent from the gate report's changed-files list, and agent_attempt.go's diff is +7 lines (the WrapRunOnly hook only), so the failure-path stdout write predates this change. Advisory because pre-existing issues never block.

message: Acceptance criterion #11 ('pinned by a test that walks the emitted affordances') has a hole: TestEmittedAffordancesUseCurrentGrammar walks real emitted values only for the typed precondition errors and the contract_draft stage of nextCommandsForRun. The executed/gated/review_prepared/review_approved/memory_proposed branches (resolve.go:266-275, nextReviewCommands resolve.go:306-321) and the literal next values in ContractDraft, ReviewRun, ReviewFix, and the multi-run status branch are covered only by hand-typed duplicate strings in the test's collect(...) block, and no behavioral test asserts next for those surfaces. A typo or stale verb introduced in those production branches would pass go test ./... while emitting a broken affordance to agents. The duplicated literals match production today, so this is a pinning gap, not a live bug.
evidence: affordances_test.go:373-378 walks nextCommandsForRun only at contract_draft; lines 384-400 hand-type the remaining stage commands. grep across all *_test.go shows no other test asserts next/Next values (only human 'Next:' text in prompt_test.go:137, task_clarify_test.go:206). Marked blocking per the if-unsure rule: this is the mechanism the contract's criterion #11 explicitly demands, and the gap covers half the lifecycle; the fix is a cheap extension of the existing test.

message: New conditional next-selection logic is untested on both arms: GateRun's failed-gate branch (gate.go:198-203), the WrapRunOnly exit-code/timeout conditionals in ExecuteRun (execute.go:134-140) and ReviewFix (review_fix.go:156-164), ReviewRun's runErr conditional (review.go:644-650), and the nextReviewCommands gating on pending proposals / open blocking findings (resolve.go:306-321). Existing JSON tests run these commands and decode the wrapped responses but never assert next, so an inverted condition would pass the suite.
evidence: execute_test.go:623/683, gate_test.go:202, review_test.go:1282/1602 exercise the commands in --json mode without asserting next. Advisory: ReviewApprove independently rejects open blocking findings (review.go:565), so a wrong affordance errors rather than corrupting state, and the contract only mandated representative pinning.

message: workflow.md claims all read-only `show` commands with a missing artifact return `pactum.not_ready.v1`, but `pactum prompt show --json` on an unbuilt prompt emits promptShowNotBuiltResponse (internal/app/prompt.go:86-95) which has no `schema` field at all. Agents dispatching on schema == "pactum.not_ready.v1" will misclassify this response. Same claim in docs/agent-skill.md:57-59. The shape is pre-existing; the over-general doc sentence is new in this change.
evidence: internal/app/prompt.go:183-190 writes promptShowNotBuiltResponse{RunID, RunStatus, Ready:false, Message, SuggestedCommand, Fix} with no Schema field, while gate/memory/review/contract-draft show route through writeNotReady which sets schema pactum.not_ready.v1 (internal/app/readiness.go).

message: nextCommandsForRun can advertise pactum review approve <run_id> for a review prepared from a failed gate report, but ReviewApprove rejects failed gates.
evidence: nextReviewCommands returns review approve when there are no pending proposals or blocking findings, but internal/app/review.go rejects approval when gateReport.Status == "failed".

message: Pending review proposals collected after review approval can leave next pointing at pactum memory propose <run_id>, which MemoryPropose rejects with pending_review_proposals.
evidence: deriveRunStatus treats an approved review as review_approved without checking pending proposals, while internal/app/memory.go blocks memory proposal when ProposalSummary.Pending > 0.

message: prompt show --json before the prompt is built emits a not-ready guidance response without schema pactum.not_ready.v1.
evidence: PromptShow writes promptShowNotBuiltResponse directly; that struct has no schema field even though the contract requires read-only not-ready JSON responses to keep pactum.not_ready.v1.

message: map refresh --json omits the required top-level next array for an in-scope mutating workflow-state command.
evidence: map refresh updates project map/workspace state and supports --json, but the JSON path writes MapRefreshResult directly without next, despite the contract requiring next: [] when no meaningful next action exists.

message: memory refresh --json omits the required top-level next array for an in-scope mutating workflow-state command.
evidence: MemoryRefresh appends refresh records and rewrites project memory, then writes memoryRefreshRecord directly in --json mode without next, despite the contract requiring next: [] when no meaningful next action exists.

message: The affordance grammar test hardcodes response-specific next commands instead of extracting them from the actual JSON responses, so new emitters can regress without tests failing.
evidence: Lines 384-399 manually collect literals such as pactum review proposal collect and pactum review fix apply; existing JSON tests for contract draft, review run, review fix, and execute run decode responses but do not assert the newly added Next fields.

message: nextCommandsForRun silently treats unreadable clarification records as zero open blocking questions, so JSON next can advertise pactum contract approve <run_id> even when the CLI cannot prove approval is legal.
evidence: openBlockingQuestionCount returns 0 on readClarificationQuestions/readClarificationAnswers errors, and the contract_draft branch then emits pactum contract approve instead of a safe inspection/no-next affordance.

message: The docs say `next: []` means the next move needs a human, but the implementation also emits `next: []` for terminal states such as after `pactum memory accept --json`, where there is no next move.
evidence: internal/app/resolve.go documents that a finished run has no next move, and internal/app/affordances_test.go pins memory accept as returning an empty next array.
